claim: Anthropic blocked OAuth token usage for OpenClaw via subscription accounts, restricting to Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork only
actionable: When building AI products using Claude APIs, use direct API keys instead of OAuth tokens to avoid terms-of-service violations

claim: Claude subscription is 90% cheaper per token than API pricing, creating strong incentive to circumvent API restrictions
actionable: Factor subscription vs API costs when planning deployment strategy; budget accordingly if forced to use API pricing

claim: OpenAI explicitly allows OAuth token usage with their APIs, providing a competitive advantage over Anthropic's restrictions
actionable: Evaluate OpenAI as alternative when subscription token usage is business-critical to your model

claim: OpenClaw's popularity exploded in 3 weeks, then faced trademark issue forcing name change from Claudebot to OpenClaw
actionable: When naming products, avoid close resemblance to trademarked terms even if initial product is different

claim: Context window usage of 200K+ tokens is common when agents read codebases, making API costs prohibitive without optimizations
actionable: Implement context compression and chunking strategies when building agents that process large codebases to control costs
